headline: UPDATE – Next Generation of Scientific Leaders Awarded More Than $7 Million at the 2026 Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair
summary: Hikaru Kuribayashi, 17, receives $100,000 Top Award for his creation of a simulation program to understand complex folding at the world’s largest pre-college STEM competition Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and Society for Science announced that Hikaru Kuribayashi, 17, of Sapporo, Japan won the $100,000 George D. Yancopoulos Innovator Award at the Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair 2026 (Regeneron ISEF), the world’s largest precollege science and engineering competition. Congra
